tool_args.label: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.description: Critical crosstown transportation corridor serving as a major Bronx-Manhattan connection, from Major Deegan Expressway to Boston Road
Project studying bus priority improvements on Fordham Road, including offset bus lanes and busway alternatives, presented to Fordham Road Community Advisory Board

tool_args.label: Alternative A: Offset Bus Lanes in Both Directions

payload.description: Convert existing curbside bus lanes to offset lanes and extend offset lanes along Fordham Road. In effect at all times (24/7). Bus-only lane located one-lane away from the curb. Single travel lane in both directions. Curbside lane available for pick-up/drop-off and deliveries.

Eastbound through traffic restrictions on Fordham Rd from Morris Av to Webster Av, except for buses and trucks, with local access on all blocks. Offset lane in westbound direction. Offset bus lanes as in Alternative A outside the Busway section. In effect at all times (24/7).

payload.description: Both eastbound and westbound through traffic restrictions on Fordham Rd between Jerome & Webster Aves, except for buses and trucks, with local access on all blocks. Includes offset bus lanes proposed in Alternative A outside the Busway section. In effect at all times (24/7).

tool_args.label: Fordham Rd is a Vision Zero Priority Corridor

payload.claim_text: Fordham Rd is a Vision Zero Priority Corridor with 4 VZ priority intersections at Sedgwick, University, Grand and Jerome Aves
62% of households on Bx12 corridor have no access to a private vehicle. 71% commute to work via public transit, walking, or biking.

tool_args.label: ABLE has positive effect on bus speeds

payload.claim_text: ABLE bus lane enforcement is having a positive effect on bus speeds on Fordham Road. Enforcement alone has not raised bus speeds to prior levels as blockages remain frequent.

tool_args.label: Alternative A selected as best treatment

payload.claim_text: Alternative A – offset bus lanes along Fordham Rd – is the best treatment to improve overall bus speeds, reliability, and curb demand while minimizing the effects on buses on north/south corridors

tool_args.label: Existing curbside bus lane hours on Fordham Rd

payload.claim_text: Currently only curbside bus lanes exist on Fordham Rd. Along most of the corridor, bus lanes are only in effect in peak periods, Monday-Friday, 7AM to 7PM, with a two-hour midday window for deliveries. Westbound 7 AM to Noon, and 2 PM to 7 PM. Eastbound 7-10 AM, and Noon to 7 PM.
New design addresses intense curb demand: approximately combined 150+ parking/loading spaces added to corridor instead of confined to two-hour window
83 persons killed or significantly injured between 2014-2018 between Deegan and Boston Road
